title: Signaling Error
description: Closed signaling-server error vocabulary. The wire-visible enum is exactly three values; richer reasons are surfaced only to the room owner through the authenticated `OwnerNotice` payload. Per `services/signaling/error-codes.md`. See `docs/architecture/error-schema-map.md` for the layer / consumer / owning-plan map across all error-shaped schemas.

kind.description: `wire` — joiner-visible payload (closed three-value enum). `ownerNotice` — richer payload delivered only on the room owner's authenticated channel.

wireCode.description: Required when `kind == "wire"`. `JOIN_FAILED` covers wrong code, full, expired, banned, and forbidden — every distinguishable rejection collapses to this single value. `RATE_LIMITED` is distinct only because the response carries a `retryAfterMs`. `SERVER_ERROR` is the generic 500 surface; no `cause` chain rides it.

retryAfterMs.description: Required only when `wireCode == "RATE_LIMITED"`. Always present at this position; clients MUST treat `0` as "retry permitted immediately" rather than "unknown".

ownerReason.description: Required when `kind == "ownerNotice"`. Richer reason surfaced on the authenticated owner channel. The joiner never sees this value.

ownerCount.description: Optional roll-up count delivered with `ownerNotice` when the same reason has fired multiple times since the last notice.

errorId.description: UUID v4 minted at the gateway. Cross-referenced from the on-device crash log.
